title: Quiz final — Tea time and British culture

questions.question: 'Fancy a cuppa?' means:
questions.explanation: Cuppa = cup of tea en argot. 'Fancy a cuppa?' = ça te dit un thé ?

questions.question: A 'builder's tea' is:
options: Strong tea with lots of milk and sugar
questions.explanation: Builder's tea = thé noir fort, beaucoup de lait, souvent du sucre.

questions.question: 'How do you take your tea?' asks about:
options: How you prefer it (milk, sugar, strength)
questions.explanation: Comment tu le prends = lait ? sucre ? fort ? LA question du thé.

questions.question: 'Take away' in the UK means:
questions.explanation: Take away (UK) = à emporter. To go (US).

questions.question: A 'flat white' is:
questions.explanation: Flat white = espresso + lait micro-moussé. Très tendance au UK.

questions.question: Biggest coffee chain in the UK:
questions.explanation: Costa Coffee = plus grande chaîne UK, devant Starbucks.

questions.question: Afternoon tea order (bottom to top):
questions.explanation: De bas en haut : sandwichs → scones → gâteaux (salé vers sucré).

questions.question: 'Clotted cream' is:
questions.explanation: Clotted cream = crème caillée épaisse du Devon.

questions.question: Cream first or jam first debate is between:
questions.explanation: Devon = crème d'abord. Cornwall = confiture d'abord.

questions.question: Which expresses a pre-made plan?
questions.explanation: Going to = plan/intention déjà décidé.

questions.question: At a restaurant, more natural:
options: I'm going to have the fish
Both work but 'I'll have' is more natural
questions.explanation: 'I'll have' = décision sur le moment. Plus naturel au restaurant.

questions.question: 'Shall I book a table?' means:
questions.explanation: 'Shall I...?' = voulez-vous que je... / est-ce que je devrais... ?

questions.question: Worst social crime in Britain:
questions.explanation: Resquiller = péché cardinal. Rien n'énerve plus un Britannique.

questions.question: 'That's quite interesting' likely means:
questions.explanation: 'Quite' atténue en britannique. 'Quite interesting' = moyen, pas terrible.

questions.question: On a Tube escalator:
questions.explanation: DROITE = immobile. GAUCHE = marcher. Se tromper = soupirs audibles.

questions.question: 'Not bad' in British usually means:
questions.explanation: Understatement : 'not bad' = plutôt bien, voire très bien.

questions.question: 'With respect...' actually means:
questions.explanation: Plus c'est poli, plus le désaccord est profond.

questions.question: 'I'm not being funny, but...' means:
questions.explanation: L'introduction qui annonce une critique directe.

questions.question: A 'scone' — how to pronounce:
options: Both exist — it divides the nation
questions.explanation: 'Skon' (nord) ou 'skône' (sud). Autre sujet de guerre civile.

questions.question: 'Fingers crossed' means:
questions.explanation: Fingers crossed = croisons les doigts = espérons que ça marche.
